article_meta.title: 4 Republicans defy Speaker Johnson to force House vote on extending ACA subsidies The Associated PressAP17 Dec 2025 Four

reasoning: The model classifies the article as 'Right' with 0.85 confidence. However, this is an AP wire story republished on Breitbart. The actual authorial prose is from AP and is largely neutral/Center or Lean Left in framing. The model commits several errors: (1) It relies heavily on the source being Breitbart rather than analyzing the author's prose, which is AP's Kevin Freking. (2) It commits attribution violations - 'propping up a failed marketplace' and 'spiraling health care costs' are attributed language from conservative figures, not the author's own prose. (3) The author's own prose choices actually lean slightly left: 'stunning move,' 'soaring monthly premiums that millions of people will soon endure,' 'the more conservative wing... which has assailed the subsidies.' The actual lean of the authored prose is Center or Lean Left. Classifying it as Right is a wrong-direction error from the actual authorial lean.

custom_reasoning.classification_accuracy: The article is an AP wire story with authorial prose that uses sympathetic-to-left framing ('stunning move,' 'soaring monthly premiums that millions of people will soon endure,' describing Republican leadership as siding with 'the more conservative wing'). The actual author lean is Center to Lean Left. Classifying as 'Right' is a wrong-direction error - the model confused the publication platform (Breitbart) with the author's editorial choices.
custom_reasoning.confidence_calibration: The model assigns 0.85 confidence to a 'Right' classification that is wrong in direction. The actual AP prose is Center/Lean Left, making this a severely miscalibrated confidence on a wrong classification.
custom_reasoning.reasoning_evidence_quality: Multiple attribution violations: 'propping up a failed marketplace' is attributed to the conservative wing, not the author; 'spiraling health care costs' is Thune's language. The model also relies on source reputation (Breitbart) rather than analyzing the author's actual prose. One indicator ('Headline emphasizes defiance') is somewhat valid as an editorial choice, but the headline is also AP's, not Breitbart's editorial framing.
